title: Paul Thomas Anderson Wins Best Director at 2026 Golden Globes
text: Director Paul Thomas Anderson claimed the Best Director award at the 2026 Golden Globes for his film "One Battle After Another," extending his winning streak after also capturing both Best Director and Best Screenplay at the Critics Choice Awards the previous week. Anderson expressed gratitude for the recognition, calling the win "generous" and thanking attendees for their "affection" toward his work. He delivered particularly emotional remarks about two important figures in his career. The director praised Warner Bros. chairperson Mike De Luca for his long-standing support spanning approximately 25-30 years. Anderson noted that De Luca "wanted to be my champion and he single handedly supported me in movies that I wanted to make." He credited De Luca's vision of running a studio that allows creative freedom, stating this approach resulted in films like "Sinners" and "Weapons." Anderson dedicated the award to his late assistant director and frequent collaborator Adam Somner, who passed away from anaplastic thyroid cancer in 2024. He emphasized that Somner's role was far more significant than the title "assistant director" suggests, crediting him with making the filmmaking experience enjoyable and ultimately contributing to the film's success. One Battle After Another also earned Teyana Taylor the Golden Globe for Best Supporting Actress.
